data.title: Intel says open to clarify queries, after Chinese cyber group alleges security issue
data.description:
Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) noted that security has long been a top priority for the company and it goes to great lengths to protect its customers, following a report that the Cybersecurity Association of China asked for a security review of the American chipmaker's products sold in China.

"We look forward to working with the relevant officials on this matter to clarify any questions that may exist and demonstrate Intel's deep commitment to the safety and security of our products," said an Intel spokesperson in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

Intel's stock was up about 1% premarket on Thursday.

The CSAC, reportedly, made several allegations against Intel in a long post on its official WeChat account.

The organization cited China's national security interests for its actions. The CSAC said, "it is recommended that a network security review is initiated on the products Intel sells in China, so as to effectively safeguard China's national security and the legitimate rights and interests of Chinese consumers."

The CSAC, which is an industry group and not a government body, has close relations with the Chinese state, and this could potentially trigger a security review from the Asian country's cyberspace agency, the Cyberspace Administration of China, as according to the previous report.

data.title: Kenvue declares $0.205 dividend
data.description:
• Kenvue (NYSE:KVUE) declares $0.205/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 3.77%
  • Payable Nov. 27; for shareholders of record Nov. 13; ex-div Nov. 13.
  • See KVUE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: BRP looks to sell its Marine businesses
data.description:
• BRP (NASDAQ:DOOO) said it is looking to sell its Marine businesses namely Alumacraft, Manitou, Telwater, and Marine parts, accessories and apparel.
  • The firm added, the process will exclude all activities related to its Sea-Doo personal watercraft, Sea-Doo Switch pontoons and jet propulsion systems.
  • "After careful consideration and given the current dynamics of both the Marine and Powersports industries, we have decided to double down on our core Powersports activities and to sell our Marine businesses," said CEO, José Boisjoli.
  • The company said it expects the sale process to be completed in the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, and hence, does not anticipate any impacts on its current fiscal year 2025 guidance.

data.title: Commercial Metals GAAP EPS of $0.90, revenue of $2B
data.description:
• Commercial Metals press release (NYSE:CMC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.90.
  • Revenue of $2B (-3.8% Y/Y).
  • Consolidated core EBITDA of $227.1 million in the fourth quarter; core EBITDA margin of 11.4%
  • Solid construction activity provided stability in North America shipment volumes; margins pressured by decline in average steel pricing
  • Tensar achieved its most profitable quarter to date as a division of CMC, driving the Emerging Businesses Group adjusted EBITDA margin to 21.7% in the fourth quarter
  • Fourth quarter net earnings of $103.9 million, or $0.90 per diluted share.

data.title: KeyCorp Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.08, revenue of $695M
data.description:
• KeyCorp press release (NYSE:KEY): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.08.
  • Revenue of $695M (-55.7% Y/Y).
  • Key's estimated Common Equity Tier 1 and Tier 1 risk-based capital ratios stood at 10.8% and 12.6%, respectively.
  • Key's tangible common equity ratio was 6.2% at September 30, 2024.

data.title: Capricor Therapeutics stock falls after pricing $75M equity offering
data.description:
• Capricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ:CAPR) fell 12.2% in premarket trade, after its public offering to raise $75M at $17 apiece.
  • The shares are being offering at a discount of 17% as of its last close on Wednesday.
  • Under the underwritten offering, the firm will offer 4.4M shares of common stock.

data.title: Biden to forgive additional $4.5B in student loans
data.description:
The Biden administration said on Thursday that it is forgiving an additional $4.5B in student loans for over 60,000 public service workers through its Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF) program.

The latest relief brings the number of public service workers who have had their student loans cancelled to over 1M people during the current administration.

“In total, the Biden-Harris Administration has approved $175B in student debt relief for nearly 5 million borrowers through various actions,” the White House said in a briefing.

The PSLF program is a U.S. government program that enables public service workers to have their federal student loans cancelled after a decade in repayment. The government today also announced a series of new steps to encourage public workers to take advantage of the PSLF program.

Tickers related to student loans: SoFi Technologies (SOFI), Navient (NAVI), Nelnet (NNI), SLM Corp. (SLM).
